title: Mechanize the instruction-layer audit, then fix the six findings it turns red — so audit 4 finds nothing
action: THREE audits have now each found gaps the previous one missed (7, then 1+1, then 6), and the third named the reason: there is NO derived gate over the instruction surfaces, so the chain stays green on every finding and the next audit finds an eleventh. Stop hand-auditing. BUILD THE GATE FIRST, RED, then fix what it names. STEP 1 — author `scripts/okf_instructions_fence.mjs`, modelled structurally on `scripts/okf_specs_fence.mjs` (read it first; mirror its shape: `--check`, `--selftest`, `--json`, gated on `bundleMode` and INERT with no bundle so a never-migrated host is unaffected, product-root-resolved, exceptions NAMED and REASONED in code and printed by --json, never a filename allowlist for the structural rule). What it enforces: in a repo WITH a bundle, any file under `skills/**`, `hooks/**`, plus `AGENTS.md`, that names the retired state layer (`docs/specs/` or `docs/history/learnings/critical-patterns.md`) must ALSO name `docs/knowledge` — i.e. it must carry a bundle branch. CLASSIFY each hit structurally, do not hand-list: (i) a LEGACY ANCHOR citation matching `docs/specs/<area>.md#<anchor>` resolves through a pointer stub and is exempt; (ii) `CREATION-LOG.md` files and anything under `docs/history/` are historical record, exempt; (iii) a file that mentions the bundle anywhere is branched, exempt; (iv) anything else is an UNBRANCHED MISROUTE and fails the chain NAMING file:line and the offending line. RED-FIRST is mandatory and is the whole point: write the classifier, run it, and CAPTURE ITS RED OUTPUT naming the six known findings below BEFORE fixing any of them. Quote that output verbatim in the evidence. STEP 2 — fix what it names. The third audit's six, each verified present: (P1) `hooks/bee-session-close.mjs` capture-nudge string says the newest decision is newer than 'every area spec under docs/specs/' and tells the agent to 'merge it into the touched area's spec' — unconditionally, with NO bundle branch, even though the surrounding LOGIC is bundle-aware (it maxes docs/specs against docs/knowledge), so in a migrated repo it fires precisely because the BUNDLE is stale and then names the wrong target — which `okf_specs_fence` would then fail the chain for obeying. Mirror what f4-3 did for the session-INIT preamble; this is the close half nobody looked at. Check whether `.bee/bin/hooks/bee-session-close.mjs` is a mirrored copy and keep both consistent. (P2) `skills/bee-planning/SKILL.md:29` cites 'AGENTS.md startup step 5 (read critical-patterns before planning) is unchanged' — the number is wrong (it is step 6) AND 'is unchanged' is now false, since f4-5 branched it. (P2) `skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md:36` bootstrap mode trigger is unbranched and is contradicted by its OWN reference at `scribing-reference.md:188` ('No-bundle only. A repo with a bundle has no equivalent bootstrap'). (P3) `skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md` (~67, 167, 187), `skills/bee-exploring/SKILL.md:29`, `skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md:34` state critical-patterns as mandatory pre-work with no bundle branch. (P3) `skills/bee-grooming/references/grooming-reference.md:20` keys the stale-spec entropy rule unconditionally to docs/specs frontmatter while its own SKILL.md branches. (P3) `skills/bee-scribing/SKILL.md:206` requires UI snapshots under `docs/specs/visuals/`, which `okf_specs_fence` would fail as new content in bundle mode — and bundle mode names no alternative home, so state the bundle branch AND record the missing home as an Open Gap rather than inventing one. STEP 3 — wire the gate into the chain: add BOTH `--selftest` and `--check` entries to `EXTRA_SUITES` in `scripts/run_verify.mjs` and to `MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS` in `scripts/test_verify_manifest.mjs`, exactly as f3-4 did for the specs fence, and raise `SUITE_FLOOR_COUNT` accordingly. STEP 4 — because you touch `skills/**`, run `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs` then `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`; if you touch anything under `templates/lib` or `.bee/bin`, also mirror-copy and re-run self-onboard. The FULL chain is your LAST action and must pass BOTH with and without the BEE_AGENT_NAME prefix.

truths: RED evidence: the new fence's output BEFORE any fix, naming the six findings with file:line — quoted verbatim
GREEN evidence: node scripts/okf_instructions_fence.mjs --check reports zero unbranched misroutes
the fence --selftest proves it BITES: a fixture file naming docs/specs with no bundle mention FAILS, and each of the three exemption classes (legacy anchor, historical log, branched file) PASSES
the fence is INERT in a bundle-less fixture — a never-migrated host is unaffected and cannot tell this shipped
the capture nudge names the resolved target, and its bundle branch is asserted by a test
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0
prohibitions: never recognise exemptions by a hand-maintained filename list — classify structurally, exactly as the specs fence does
do not fix any of the six findings before the fence has been run RED against them and its output captured
do not invent a bundle home for UI visuals — state the gap, do not fill it
do not weaken okf_specs_fence or any existing assertion to make the new gate pass

trace.outcome: The instruction layer now has a gate, so the fourth audit is a machine and not a person. Three hand audits each found what the last missed (7, then 2, then 6) while the chain stayed green through all of them, because nothing tested prose. scripts/okf_instructions_fence.mjs closes that: in bundle mode, any line under skills/**/*.md, hooks/** or AGENTS.md that names the retired state layer must carry the branch on that line, with three structural exemptions (legacy anchor citations, historical record, already-branched lines) — never a filename allowlist — and it is inert with no bundle so a never-migrated host cannot tell it shipped. Run RED first against pristine HEAD it named 24 misroutes in 10 files, including the P1 nobody had looked at: the session-CLOSE capture nudge, whose logic was bundle-aware but whose message told the agent to write into the tree another guard fails the chain for accepting. Nine of the 24 had been found by no audit at all. The decisive finding is evidence against the cell's own spec: a FILE-level rule was implemented first, measured, and discarded because it scored GREEN on four of the six known misroutes — a branch three paragraphs above a bullet does not travel with the bullet an agent actually reads. UI visuals have no bundle home; that is recorded as an Open Gap, not invented. Chain 81 suites, green both with and without the agent-name prefix.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "The gate was BUILT AND RUN RED BEFORE ANY FIX, against a pristine `git archive` of HEAD 9166943 — reproducible, and preserved verbatim at `docs/history/okf-integration-close-f4/reports/red-instructions-fence.md`. It reported 24 unbranched misroutes across 10 files, naming file:line and quoting each offending line. Five of the third audit's six hand-found findings are in that list, INCLUDING the P1: `hooks/bee-session-close.mjs:168` — 'area spec under docs/specs/ — a settled outcome may exist only in the decision log'. The sixth (bee-planning/SKILL.md:29's 'startup step 5 ... is unchanged') carries no path token and is structurally invisible to any path fence; it was fixed anyway and that limit is stated rather than hidden. NINE of the 24 had been found by no audit at all: compounding-reference:77, grooming-reference:55/56, bee-scribing/SKILL.md:162, and scribing-reference 24/26/155/232/284.",
  "the_design_finding": "THE most important result, and it is evidence against the cell's own wording. The worker first implemented exemption (iii) exactly as the cell specified it — 'a file that mentions the bundle anywhere is branched' — ran it, and measured that it scored GREEN on FOUR OF THE SIX misroutes the human audit had found by hand. `bee-hive/SKILL.md` names the bundle four times and still called critical-patterns.md 'mandatory pre-work reading'; `bee-session-close.mjs` is bundle-aware in its logic and still printed 'merge it into the touched area's spec'. A file-level rule is provably blind to the exact failure this gate was commissioned to catch. The shipped rule is therefore LINE-LOCAL: a line naming the retired layer must carry the branch ON THAT LINE. The justification is how the failure actually happens — an agent reads a bullet, a table row, or a hook message in isolation, and a branch three paragraphs above does not travel with it. The discarded file-level implementation is the evidence, not an assertion.",
  "green_evidence": "`node scripts/okf_instructions_fence.mjs` -> PASS: 22 instruction-surface file(s) name the retired state layer (14 branched, 4 historical-record, 4 legacy-anchor), 0 unbranched misroutes. Re-run independently by the orchestrator.",
  "selftest_proves_it_bites": "23 rows, 0 failed, re-run independently by the orchestrator. It proves: an unbranched line FAILS naming file:line and quoting it; the fence is INERT with no bundle (identical fixture minus one parsing concept -> inert:true, zero findings, does not even scan) so a never-migrated host cannot tell this shipped; a `.gitkeep`-only tree is not a bundle; the divorced topology resolves the predicate off the PRODUCT root while the surfaces stay at the harness root. Each of the three required exemption classes passes AND has a NEGATIVE TWIN: legacy-anchor accepts both `#B9a` and bare `R17` forms but is per-hit, so a misroute on the next line still fires, and a near-miss ('<file>.md Business Rules') does NOT pass; historical-record covers CREATION-LOG.md and docs/history/ paths; branched-line accepts both markers. One row explicitly asserts the anti-file-level property: a file naming the bundle ELSEWHERE does not buy an unbranched line a pass.",
  "p1_fixed_and_asserted": "The capture nudge now routes on the same ONE predicate as its session-INIT twin and names the resolved target — bundle: 'author it as a concept in the touched area's bundle folder'; no bundle: BYTE-IDENTICAL to what shipped. The perverse mechanic is now recorded in the code comment: the surrounding logic already maxed docs/specs against the bundle, so in a migrated repo the nudge fires precisely BECAUSE THE BUNDLE IS STALE, and then sent the agent to a tree that okf_specs_fence fails the chain for accepting new content — an agent obeying one guard would have been stopped by another. Asserted by two new end-to-end rows in hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs whose fixtures differ only by one parsing concept; the no-bundle row pins the pre-change wording as an exact literal. Proven to bite: swapping HEAD's hook back in turns the bundle row FAIL with the old string in the output. The `.bee/bin/hooks/` mirror was byte-identical and is kept so.",
  "chain_wiring": "Both `--selftest` and the bare check wired into EXTRA_SUITES; the exact path added to MANDATORY_SUITES and `--selftest` to MANDATORY_SUITE_ARGS, exactly as f3-4 did for the specs fence; SUITE_FLOOR_COUNT 62 -> 64. Discovery now reports 81 suites.",
  "chain": "Re-run INDEPENDENTLY by the orchestrator, not accepted on the worker's word: `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs` -> PASS 81 suite(s) exit 0; `node scripts/run_verify.mjs` -> PASS 81 suite(s) exit 0. Both ways green is the standing property f4-4 established.",
  "deviations_accepted": "Five, each stated by the worker with its reason and each reviewed. (1) Line-local instead of file-level exemption (iii) — accepted on the measured evidence above; without it the gate cannot see 4 of 6 known findings. (2) Scope narrowed from `skills/**` to `skills/**/*.md`: non-markdown under skills/ is shipped machinery where a retired path is DATA (fixture paths, JSDoc examples, constants the onboarder emits), and the literal reading produced 100+ hits demanding cosmetic edits to fixtures; the narrowing is declared in --json as a surface with its reason. (3) `hooks/test_*.mjs` excluded via run_verify's own suite glob, no new convention invented — a suite's retired-layer strings are its assertions, and the suite pinning this very hook's no-bundle wording verbatim would otherwise be a misroute against itself. (4) Two extra exemption classes, each measured against the pre-fix tree: `branched-section` covers exactly ONE line tree-wide — bee-scribing/SKILL.md:108, whose text is pinned VERBATIM by test_bundle_mode, so branching it inline would have meant weakening an existing assertion, which the cell forbids; `fenced-example` covers two illustrations. Neither changes any other verdict. (5) The canonical RED capture uses the final classifier against pristine HEAD; an earlier mid-development capture read 27 before per-line dedup.",
  "open_gap_not_filled": "The bundle defines no home for UI visual snapshots. The prohibition said state the gap, do not invent a home — honored: it is recorded as an Open Gap in areas/okf-profile/concept-model-and-authoring.md, and bee-scribing now requires a bundle-mode UI area to record its missing snapshot as an Open Gap in its own concept. Deciding a home is a profile question (is a binary asset a concept?) that no decision has settled.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Fence selftest and check re-run; both verify invocations re-run; the P1 diff read in full. One flagged diagnostic investigated and dismissed as a false positive: `runCaptureNudgeRows` was reported as declared-but-never-read, which would have meant the new assertions never execute — it is invoked at hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:3649 via `results.push(...await runCaptureNudgeRows())`, which the language server does not follow. The new rows do run."
}
